page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU allocation is shared. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. Storage is bundled as a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores align with the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including read-only small block cached operations at 1034.93 GB/sec and large block uncached writes at 69.27 GB/sec, fall within the average range. Database operations and Redis SET performance also register average tier results, with Redis achieving 15.09 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference using a small 135M model yields average speeds, whereas prompt processing on a medium 7B model drops to the bottom 25% tier.

The shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU alongside a substantial 1920 GB NVMe SSD. However, the shared CPU model introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-core loads. This hardware profile is suited for memory-optimized and storage-heavy workloads such as relational databases, caching layers, static web serving, and general-purpose applications that require significant local storage but do not demand continuous, dedicated CPU performance.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 8 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM. It includes 1920 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads, though it introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs under sustained multi-core utilization. This instance is suited for database hosting, caching layers, static web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring high storage capacity and memory density.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered instance with 8 shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering top-tier single-core performance for database and memory-heavy workloads.

tagline: An AMD EPYC instance combining high memory density and local NVMe storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.
